_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize TX state-legislature batch: donna-campbell, jose-menendez, matt-morgan, adam-hinojosa, ron-reynolds. State tier (49 cells). Every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence; never inferred from party. Campbell & Adam Hinojosa won 4yr Senate terms in 2024 -> NOT on 2026 ballot (candidacy_status corrected to incumbent).

donna-campbell@TX.notes_set: Texas Senate District 25 (R), in office since 2013; won 4-yr term Nov 2024 (term to Jan 2029, NOT on 2026 ballot). Uniformly conservative record. Pro-life: Texas Alliance for Life 100% (89th Leg.) + Courageous Defense of Life Award, sponsored SB33 (no taxpayer-funded abortion travel), pro-life PAC endorsement. Marriage/sexuality: authored SB14 (88R) banning transition care for minors. Family: voted YES on SB2 (89R) ESA school choice. Christian liberty: floor advocacy + majority vote for SB10 Ten Commandments in classrooms. 2A: supported HB1927 permitless carry, served as Senate conferee. No left/non-conservative deviations found.

0.note: Texas Alliance for Life 100% (89th Leg.); sponsored SB33; consistent pro-life record affirming protection of life.

4.note: Texas Alliance for Life PAC endorsement; no abortion-industry (PP/NARAL/EMILY's List) money found.

2.note: Authored SB14 (88R, 2023) banning puberty blockers/hormones/surgeries for minors.

0.note: Voted YES on SB2 (89R, 2025) ESA/universal school choice.

3.note: Floor advocacy + majority vote for SB10 (89R) Ten Commandments in public-school classrooms.

0.note: Supported HB1927 (87R, 2021) permitless/constitutional carry; served as Senate conferee.

jose-menendez@TX.notes_set: Texas Senate District 26 (D, San Antonio), in office since 2015; term to Jan 2027, running for reelection (2026 Dem primary). Consistently progressive on life/marriage/school choice/religious liberty/election integrity. DEVIATION (cross-party credit): pro-law-enforcement -- CLEAT 'Senator of the Year', pro-officer legislation. Documented CONTRARY: Planned Parenthood 'Legislative Star'/endorsed + abortion-expansion sponsorships; opposed SB14 (trans minor care ban) and SB15 (trans athletes); voted against ESA school vouchers ('rooted in segregation'); opposed SB10 Ten Commandments; opposed Texas voter ID and anti-mail-ballot-fraud penalties; voted NO on HB1927 permitless carry ('irresponsible').

0.note: Planned Parenthood 'Legislative Star'; sponsored abortion-expansion bills; rejects personhood framing.

1.note: Voted against banning public abortion funding and against a third-trimester ban; advocates abortion access.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes (named 'Legislative Star') -- aligned with abortion industry.

2.note: Opposed SB14 (2023) ban on gender transition for minors; offered amendments to weaken it.

4.note: Spoke against SB15 (2023) limiting trans athletes in women's collegiate sports.

0.note: Voted against SB1 ESA/school-choice vouchers (2023 special session); called vouchers 'rooted in segregation.'

3.note: Opposed SB10 (2025) Ten Commandments in schools; security-procedure amendment failed 10-20.

1.note: Opposed Texas voter ID law as designed to 'disenfranchise'/'oppress' voters.

3.note: Opposed raising penalties for false mail-ballot applications.

0.note: DEVIATION (cross-party): CLEAT 'Senator of the Year'; pro-law-enforcement legislation -- documented backing of police.
Voted NO on HB1927 (2021) permitless/constitutional carry; called it 'irresponsible.'

matt-morgan@TX.notes_set: Texas House District 26 (R, Fort Bend Co.), freshman sworn Jan 2025 (beat incumbent Jacey Jetton in 2024 R primary). Running 2026 general vs Elizabeth Markowitz (D). Thin first-session record; scored from issue-group ratings + a recorded marquee vote. Pro-life: Texas Right to Life 96, Texas Alliance for Life 93%, TRTL endorsement. Marriage/sexuality: Texas Values Action 2025 Faith & Family 94% ('Champion'), crediting the Women's Privacy Act + the man/woman-definition bill. Family: recorded YES on SB2 (ESA universal school choice) + YES to table the anti-ESA amendment. Campaign themes otherwise vague -> null. No cross-party deviation found.

0.note: Texas Right to Life 2025 score 96; Texas Alliance for Life 93%; TRTL endorsed his 2024 run.

4.note: TRTL endorsement + 96 pro-life rating; no abortion-industry money found.

2.note: Texas Values Action 2025 Faith & Family 94% ('Champion'), crediting the Women's Privacy Act (sex-separated spaces) and the bill defining 'man'/'woman' in Texas law.

0.note: Recorded YES on SB2 (2025) universal ESA/school choice; YES to table the anti-ESA referendum amendment.

adam-hinojosa@TX.notes_set: Texas Senate District 27 (R, Corpus Christi/RGV); first Republican to hold an RGV Senate seat since Reconstruction; flipped seat over Morgan LaMantia, assumed office Jan 2025; 4-yr term to Jan 2029 (NOT on 2026 ballot). Distinct from Juan 'Chuy' Hinojosa (D, SD-20). Strong conservative record via bill authorship + iVoterGuide survey. Pro-life: own statement 'I am pro-life... against abortions up until birth'; supports defunding PP; TRTL + TAL endorsements. Family/Christian liberty: AUTHOR of SB2 (ESA school choice), SB10 (Ten Commandments), SB11 (school prayer/Bible reading); Senate Select Cmte on Religious Liberty; survey-affirmed conscience exemption. Election integrity: supports voter ID + clean rolls, opposes ballot harvesting + electronic-only voting. Border: member Senate Border Security Cmte, 'strongly agree' to physically secure the border. 2A: favors no gun restrictions. One soft flag: declined the gender-identity-protected-class survey question (omission, not contrary).

0.note: Own statement: 'I am pro-life and fully against... abortions all the way up until birth'; iVoterGuide: abortion only to preserve mother's life; supports defunding PP.

4.note: Endorsed by Texas Right to Life PAC and Texas Alliance for Life; supports defunding Planned Parenthood; no abortion-industry money.

0.note: Listed AUTHOR of SB2 (89R) Texas Education Freedom Act / ESA school choice; signed into law May 2025.

1.note: iVoterGuide: 'Strongly Disagree' that businesses must provide services violating religious beliefs -- supports conscience exemptions.

3.note: AUTHOR of SB10 (Ten Commandments in classrooms) and SB11 (period of prayer/Bible reading in public schools), both signed 2025.

0.note: iVoterGuide: opposes electronic-only voting; supports chain-of-custody (paper trail).

1.note: iVoterGuide: supports voter ID and clean voter rolls.

0.note: iVoterGuide: 'Strongly Agree -- the U.S. should do more to physically secure the southern border'; member, Senate Committee on Border Security (89R).
iVoterGuide: favors 'None' for gun restrictions; 'freedom to keep and bear arms is protected' -- supports permitless carry.

1.note: iVoterGuide: favors no gun restrictions; opposes lawsuits against manufacturers -- opposes red-flag/mag-limit/AWB regime.

ron-reynolds@TX.notes_set: Texas House District 27 (D, Missouri City/Fort Bend Co.), in office since 2011; running for reelection (2026 Dem primary). Consistently progressive -- Texas Values Action 2025 Faith & Family 0% (18 bills), Texans for Fiscal Responsibility career 'F'. Documented CONTRARY: Planned Parenthood Texas Votes endorsement + pro-abortion votes/statements; voted against HB229 'What is a Woman Act' & SB1257 detransitioners, voted FOR HB1738 repealing the homosexual-conduct statute; voted against SB2 ESA vouchers and against SB13 (removing harmful library books); voted against SB10 Ten Commandments, SB11 prayer, SB965 religious-liberty; opposed SB1 election-integrity law (broke quorum to block it) and lobbied Congress for federal voting-rights preemption. No cross-party (conservative) deviation found. (Note: 2015 misdemeanor barratry conviction; remained seated -- not disqualifying.)

0.note: Rejects personhood; member of the Reproductive Freedom Leadership Council; voted No on SB8 (heartbeat) and HB1280 (provider penalties).

1.note: Own statement: committed to 'restore, protect, and expand access to... abortion.'

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes (requires 100% abortion-access commitment).

2.note: Voted against HB229 ('What is a Woman Act') and SB1257 (Detransitioners Bill of Rights), 2025.

4.note: Voted FOR HB1738 repealing the homosexual-conduct statute -- pro-LGBTQ policy position.

0.note: Voted against SB2 (2025) school choice/ESA vouchers.

3.note: Voted against SB13 (2025) removing 'harmful' sexual-content books from school libraries.

1.note: Voted against SB965 ('Coach Joe Kennedy' religious-liberty bill), 2025.

3.note: Voted against SB10 (Ten Commandments) and SB11 (prayer in schools), 2025.

1.note: Opposed SB1 (2021) election-integrity/voter-ID law; broke quorum and flew to DC to block it.

3.note: Opposed SB1's anti-mail-ballot-harvesting provisions; led quorum break against it.

4.note: Lobbied Congress for federal voting-rights legislation to preempt Texas election law -- favored federal involvement in state elections.
